catalysts_general: Q1 2026 earnings April 30. Street expects $595-605M +53% YoY. Q4 2025: 70% revenue growth, $252M net income, $1B buyback authorized. Director's $7M buy at higher prices. (Tier 4-5: Yahoo Finance, Nasdaq)

Discovery: Director Sarah Farrell purchased ~50,500 shares ($7M) in Feb 2026 via Waygrove Partnership after post-IPO decline; Google data partnership driving advertising revenue growth; conviction buy by a board-level insider.
value_picks.analyst_take: 70% revenue growth; 91% gross margins; $1B buyback; Q1 earnings April 30 (tomorrow); AI data licensing largely unmonetized

catalysts_general: AXON 2 AI optimizer confirmed 200%+ ROAS for advertisers. Q1 2026 May 6. Forecast $1.78B revenue, 56.6% annual EPS growth. Analyst target $647 (+41%). Down 38% from $745 ATH. (Tier 4-5: MarketBeat, ValuSense)
value_picks.analyst_take: AXON 2 AI optimizer driving 200%+ ROAS; 88% gross margins, 77% op margins near-unique in ad-tech; 38% off ATH; Q1 May 6

catalysts_general: FY2026 revenue guidance raised to $5B. Trust-Based Solutions +125% YoY. Analyst targets $25.50-$46. Consumer cybersecurity defensive moat. (Tier 4: MarketBeat)
value_picks.analyst_take: Fwd P/E 6.6 with $1.3B FCF; Trust-Based Solutions +125% YoY; FY2026 guidance raised to $5B; D/E 363 limits sizing

catalysts_general: Record Q2 revenue $268M +272% YoY. FY2026 173% revenue growth, 301% EPS growth projected. $750M DustPhotonics acquisition for silicon photonics. 224G SerDes chiplets for 1.6Tbps infrastructure. (Tier 1: BusinessWire, Seeking Alpha)
value_picks.analyst_take: 272% revenue growth; 1.6Tbps 224G SerDes chiplets for AI networking; $750M DustPhotonics silicon photonics acquisition

catalysts_general: Morningstar wide-moat upgrade with FVE $285 (+57% upside) citing 34% discount to fair value. Life sciences recovery thesis. $4.6B FCF. Slow-burn value play without near-term catalyst. (Tier 5: Morningstar)

Discovery: Morningstar recently upgraded moat rating to wide and raised FVE to $285; stock trades at a 34% discount to that estimate, with expected 13% EPS CAGR through 2028 as life-sciences instruments recover.
value_picks.analyst_take: Morningstar wide-moat FVE $285; 34% discount; Fwd P/E 20; FCF $4.6B; life sciences recovery underway

catalysts_general: OTTO AMR launch drives intelligent factory automation. Q1 segment earnings +36% YoY. AI-driven industrial automation secular demand. Re-shoring tailwind from tariff policy. (Tier 4: industry coverage)
value_picks.analyst_take: OTTO AMR autonomous mobile robot launch; Q1 segment earnings +36% YoY; industrial AI automation secular demand; re-shoring tailwind

catalysts_general: DOD contract-for-difference price floor at $110/kg + $400M preferred at $30.03 conversion (well in-the-money at $62). NdPr spot hit $111.5-126/kg in Q1, up 138% YTD. 10X heavy rare earth facility targeting mid-2026. Wedbush raised to $90. (Tier 1-3: MP IR, DOD announcement, Wedbush)

Discovery: Iran war disrupted sulfur flows through the Strait of Hormuz (nearly half of global sulfur trade passes through), tightening supply of the feedstock for rare earth processing — while simultaneously Trump's Project Vault ($12B critical minerals reserve) is boosting domestic rare earth demand; MP Materials' 10X Facility (heavy rare earth separation) targets mid-2026 commissioning, and NdPr oxide prices surged to $90,000/tonne; Wedbush raised target to $90 in April 2026 with the vertical integration story 'not yet fully priced in'.
value_picks.analyst_take: DOD 10-year NdPr price floor ($110/kg) + 15% equity stake via $400M preferred stock eliminates downside; Iran war tightening global rare earth supply

catalysts_general: Q1 2026: AUM $1.3T +12% YoY, distributable earnings +25% to $1.76B, infrastructure +41% YoY. Buy consensus avg target $158 (+31%). BCRED retail outflows minor vs institutional platform strength. (Tier 4: Yahoo Finance, GuruFocus)
value_picks.analyst_take: Record Q1 AUM $1.3T, distributable earnings +25%; infrastructure +41% YoY; 37% off highs with fundamental acceleration

catalysts_general: Q1 2026: EPS $1.23 vs $0.76 est, revenue $12.25B beat. $25B buyback authorized — larger than entire content budget. Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan, BofA maintain Buy. Analyst consensus $114-115 (~26% above current). (Tier 4-5: Yahoo Finance, Benzinga)

Discovery: Announced a $25B stock buyback (larger than its entire $20B content budget) after a 13% share pullback driven by Reed Hastings' exit and the failed Warner Bros acquisition; Q1 showed 16% revenue growth beating estimates, and ad-tier expansion provides a durable second growth engine.
value_picks.analyst_take: $25B buyback > $20B content budget at $91 signals management conviction; Q1 EPS beat; ad-tier expansion second growth engine

catalysts_general: Organic orders +65% Q3 2025 from large hyperscaler liquid cooling programs. Data center 45% of sales targeting >50% FY26. Minnesota capacity expansion online early 2026. Q1 earnings May 1. (Tier 4-5: Nasdaq, SimplyWallSt)

Discovery: Organic orders surged ~65% in Q3 2025 driven almost entirely by large liquid cooling orders from hyperscaler programs; new Minnesota facility will double liquid cooling production footprint in early 2026, giving nVent multi-year visibility into hyperscaler demand — a mid-cap electrical infrastructure play underrepresented in AI infrastructure baskets.
value_picks.analyst_take: 65% organic orders from hyperscaler liquid cooling programs; Minnesota facility doubling capacity; data center now 45% of sales; Q1 earnings May 1

catalysts_general: Q2 FY2026 record revenue $1.13B +36% YoY, EPS $3.36 beat. Q3 earnings May 4. Building 10 (2M sq ft) late 2026, adding $2.4B capacity. Silicon photonics partnership. Analyst consensus $715. (Tier 4-5: SimplyWallSt, MarketScreener)

Discovery: Posted record Q2 FY2026 revenue of $1.13B (+36% YoY) driven by AI data center optical transceiver demand (800G/1.6T ramp); consensus pegs FY2026 at $4.55B (+33% YoY) with Building 10 expansion adding $2.4B revenue capacity — pure-play optical assembly for AI networking buildout.
value_picks.analyst_take: Only qualified high-volume assembler for 800G/1.6T optical transceivers; Building 10 doubles capacity; Q3 earnings May 4

catalysts_general: NGS ARR +33% to $6.33B; FY2026 guidance raised to $8.52-8.62B. Platform consolidation 1,550 customers +35%. Iranian cyberattack escalation structural demand driver. (Tier 1: PRNewswire)
value_picks.analyst_take: NGS ARR $6.33B +33%; FY2026 NGS guide raised to $8.52-8.62B (53-54%); platform consolidation 1,550 customers; Iranian cyberattacks structural demand tailwind

catalysts_general: Record $22B Q2 bookings, book-to-bill 1.2x. $5B+ generative AI consulting bookings. 46% drawdown from $325 ATH. Morgan Stanley trimmed on margin compression fears. (Tier 4: Trefis, AInvest)
value_picks.analyst_take: Record $22B Q2 bookings book-to-bill 1.2x; $5B+ GenAI consulting bookings; 46% drawdown from ATH; slow-burn value play

catalysts_general: VLCC spot day rates at ~$400,000/day ATH from Hormuz disruption. Jefferies Buy $90 target. Low net debt versus peers. Ceasefire binary risk — tight stop. (Tier 4: Jefferies via 247WallSt)

Discovery: The 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis (traffic down ~70% in March) drove VLCC spot rates to $423,000/day — unprecedented in 20+ years of data — and INSW guided a record Q1 2026; the company's low net debt-to-EBITDA (~1.1x) vs peers gives it financial flexibility to capitalize on extended disruption while Jefferies initiated at Buy with a $90 PT; peace talks stalled again as of April 27, keeping the rate premium alive.
value_picks.analyst_take: Hormuz closure driving VLCC day rates to ATH; Jefferies Buy $90; low net debt; ceasefire risk constrains sizing

catalysts_general: Similar thesis to FRO — Hormuz disruption driving VLCC day rates. P/E 7.8. Near ATH. Ceasefire binary negative risk — prefer entry on pullback. (Tier 4: industry data)

Discovery: Teekay Tankers' Suezmax and Aframax fleet is directly benefiting from the Hormuz-driven rerouting of crude oil flows (tonne-mile demand surge), with management guiding a record Q1 2026 and spot rates at multi-decade highs; the stock trades at a lower market cap than peers yet has a leveraged earnings structure that amplifies spot rate upside — and the peace talks breakdown on April 27 extends the geopolitical rate premium into Q2.
value_picks.analyst_take: Hormuz VLCC rates ATH; P/E 7.8 historically cheap; similar geopolitical tailwind to FRO

catalysts_general: New CFO bought $1.6M personal shares. AV partnerships with Waymo/Aurora create optionality. Fwd P/E 17.8. Analyst consensus ~$100-105. (Tier 4: MarketBeat)

Discovery: Newly promoted CFO Balaji Krishnamurthy spent $1.6M on his first open-market purchase (22,453 shares) just days after taking the CFO role in Feb 2026, a strong signal of personal conviction in the company's autonomous-vehicle monetization path.
value_picks.analyst_take: New CFO $1.6M personal buy signals conviction; AV monetization optionality; Fwd P/E 17.8; $105 analyst target

catalysts_general: Q1 FY2027 product revenue guidance $1.262-1.267B +27% YoY. 45/52 analysts Buy. OpenAI GPT-5.5 and Claude integrations converting to workloads. Mean analyst target $233-248 implies 60-70% upside. FCF $1.6B. (Tier 4-5: TIKR, Trefis)

Discovery: AI data cloud platform down 48.6% from 52-week high with 45 of 51 analysts maintaining Buy/Strong Buy; recent OpenAI GPT-5.5 integration into Snowflake's platform creates a new enterprise AI data layer catalyst — 9,100+ accounts already using Snowflake AI features; Q1 FY2027 earnings on May 20 is the binary catalyst where product revenue acceleration and AI workload growth could force consensus upgrades; RSI dipped to oversold territory in April and has since stabilized; mean analyst target of $233-$248 implies 65-72% upside.
value_picks.analyst_take: 45/52 analysts Buy despite 48% drawdown; AI workload growth converting OpenAI/Claude integrations; Q1 earnings May 20; $233-248 analyst consensus

catalysts_general: Q3 FY2026: DC sales +78% YoY, adj EPS $1.19 beat. Raised full-year guidance +20-25% net sales, targeting $2B DC revenue FY28. Strong Buy consensus. (Tier 4: Trefis, Yahoo Finance)

Discovery: Data center sales rose 31% sequentially in Q3 FY2026 with quarterly revenues exceeding $400M; management guided 50–70% annual data center growth for the next two fiscal years and record intake has extended demand visibility to nearly five years — a small-cap thermal management pure-play with hyperscaler program depth rarely covered by Street analysts.
value_picks.analyst_take: Data center sales +78% YoY; targeting $2B DC revenue by FY28; Strong Buy consensus; liquid cooling for AI thermal management

catalysts_general: $250M buyback authorized April 23. 27% Q1 revenue growth. Near 52-week low. ADAS technology as Intel spinoff with clean balance sheet. (Tier 4: MarketBeat)
value_picks.analyst_take: $250M buyback authorized April 23; 27% Q1 revenue growth; near 52-week low; ADAS technology re-rating potential

catalysts_general: ASCO plenary data presentation pending. HARMONi-3 interim analysis. $510K call sweep at near-money strikes. Analyst target $33.71. Biotech binary event risk. (Tier 4: options flow data)

Discovery: April 16 call sweep: $510K in calls at $27 strike (7/17/26) with size exceeding prior OI, timed ahead of Q2 2026 HARMONi-3 interim PFS analysis for ivonescimab in NSCLC and ASCO plenary presentation of HARMONi-6 data (selected April 21) — dual catalysts in a 90-day window with PDUFA date November 14, 2026.
value_picks.analyst_take: ASCO plenary HARMONi-3 interim data; $510K call sweep April 16; $33.71 analyst target; small position only

catalysts_general: PDUFA May 29 for pediatric Afrezza inhaled insulin label expansion. $7 analyst target implies 161% upside. Speculative binary event. Small position only. (Tier 4: MarketBeat)

Discovery: FDA PDUFA decision on Afrezza inhaled insulin for pediatric diabetes (ages 4-17) is due May 29, 2026; if approved it would be the first inhaled rapid-acting insulin for children, a significant label expansion for an existing commercial product.
value_picks.analyst_take: PDUFA May 29 pediatric Afrezza FDA decision; analyst target $7 (+161%); small speculative position only given binary nature

catalysts_general: MUX TACAIR/CCA award confirmed: Northrop-Kratos USMC loyal wingman team $231.5M OTA. $1.45B MACH-TB 2.0 hypersonic test services contract. FY2026 guidance $1.595-1.675B. Q1 earnings May 6 (weak due to CRA timing). (Tier 2: Breaking Defense, GlobeNewswire)

Discovery: Kratos was selected by Northrop as airframe partner for the Marine Corps MUX TACAIR Collaborative Combat Aircraft program (XQ-58 Valkyrie drone wingman), won a $447M space systems contract, and received counter-UAS orders — all in Q1 2026 — as the Iran war and NATO rearmament accelerate unmanned systems demand; EPS consensus up 40% YoY for 2026.
value_picks.analyst_take: $231.5M CCA Northrop-Kratos USMC loyal wingman award + $1.45B MACH-TB hypersonic; analyst median $115 (+84%)

catalysts_general: FY2026 guidance cut by $75M but LOCUST laser weapon demonstrated. Analyst median $305 (+58%). Buy consensus. Drone/autonomous defense structural demand. (Tier 4: Motley Fool)
value_picks.analyst_take: LOCUST laser weapon demonstrated; analyst median $305 (+58%); autonomous drone demand structural; Buy consensus despite guidance cut
